title: 100 Greatest Indian Films of All Time — All Languages
description: The complete Navras canon — spanning Bollywood, Tamil, Telugu, Malayalam, Bengali and beyond.
intro: This is the definitive Navras list — 100 films across every Indian language that represent the absolute best this country's cinema has to offer. No favouritism, no star worship. Just films that endure.

entries.blurb: India's most magnificent epic. A landmark in world cinema.

film.title: Mughal-E-Azam
film.verdict: India's most magnificent epic. A landmark in world cinema.

entries.blurb: Satyajit Ray's debut. Still the finest Indian film ever made.

film.title: Pather Panchali
film.verdict: Best Human Document, Cannes 1956. Where it all began.

entries.blurb: Kamal Haasan's Godfather. One of India's greatest films.

film.title: Nayakan
film.verdict: India's answer to The Godfather. Kamal Haasan's greatest.

entries.blurb: The film that gave Indian romance its modern face.

film.title: Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge
film.verdict: The film that gave Indian romance its modern face.

entries.blurb: The perfect Malayalam thriller. Mohanlal at his finest.

film.title: Drishyam
film.verdict: The perfect thriller. Remade five times. None come close.

entries.blurb: Ray's most perfectly realised film. Heartbreaking and beautiful.

film.title: Charulata
film.verdict: Ray's most perfectly realised film. Heartbreaking and beautiful.

film.title: Lagaan
film.verdict: Nominated for Best Foreign Film, Oscars 2002. India's greatest Oscar moment.

entries.blurb: India's most spectacular blockbuster. A modern epic.

film.title: Baahubali 2
film.verdict: India's most spectacular blockbuster. A modern epic.

entries.blurb: The film that proved Indian sport could power world cinema.

film.title: Dangal
film.verdict: The film that proved Indian sport could power world cinema.

entries.blurb: India's most complete crime epic. Five hours of brilliance.

film.title: Gangs of Wasseypur
film.verdict: If you haven't seen it, stop everything and watch it now.
